behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: this is mainly parser/resolver handoff, not external infra. Pass-2 challenge: System rejected because behavior text is about extraction/inclusion correctness, not real network/subprocess/OS effects; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI step. Unit is too low when transitive resolution crosses modules (manifest parser + dependency graph resolver + check-input builder).
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency-discovery flow against fixture manifests/lockfiles (e.g., package.json + lock, requirements + lock) and assert the vulnerability-check input contains the full expected set of direct and transitive packages/versions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: version-range correctness could be tested with a stub. Pass-2 challenge: final behavior is about DB query result correctness against exact ranges; real failure can come from real HTTP/API semver-range semantics and request encoding. Mocking can hide incompatibilities, so Integration is rejected. Agentic/Workflow rejected (no AI).
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability API sandbox/test endpoint, submit manifests with same package name but different constrained ranges and verify returned CVEs align only with affected ranges, proving request encoding + remote matching semantics are correct end-to-end.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: pure ordering logic. Pass-2 challenge: no module-boundary dependence is required; no OS/network/subprocess; no AI. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on deterministic sort rules only.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorter with mixed-severity CVE fixtures and assert strict order by severity rank, then descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: could look Unit. Pass-2 challenge: real failure usually appears in parse/edit/serialize handoff (structure preservation, key ordering, untouched fields), which is multi-module local wiring. System rejected: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update pipeline on representative manifests and assert a structural/text diff shows exactly one dependency version change and no other key/value mutations.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: external PR client boundary implies System. Pass-2 challenge confirms Integration is too low because mocks can hide real git/branch/remote API behavior and diff materialization issues. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model decision quality involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, execute the PR creation path and verify a PR exists remotely with exactly one dependency-version file change and expected branch/commit metadata.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: deterministic semver compare logic. Pass-2 challenge: no cross-module infra required for failure; Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text being pure comparison rule.
behaviors.test_description: Feed semver pairs into the comparator (including pre-release/build metadata edge cases) and assert major increments are flagged breaking while minor/patch are not.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Pass-1: orchestration behavior across semver decision, PR orchestration, and report output. Pass-2 challenge: System rejected because real remote PR infra is not required to prove skip behavior; a spy/fake PR gateway is sufficient. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI decision chain. Unit is too low because failure is in state handoff between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with a fixture that triggers breaking-change detection; assert PR gateway is not invoked and scanner output contains the review-required flag/reason in the final report object.
